entry.title: Re: Adobe Custom PickList form in SAP Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hi 

If you only enabled just the output form, that may not be enough.

I took a quick look at the BADI documentation and i think you have to consider the import parameters and changing (output) parameters to make sure your custom field is available in one of those objects

 My impression is you should also enable the Order Object Page and maybe the CDS Views, for example

PS - here is the help,  link: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/2bba750d1e124e1ea2a039bb1cd9b6c5/c20b8cfa8000468d86a005ec6d03259f.html?locale=en-US

Please confirm that is the badi (reservation one) you want to use and not for example Manufacturing Order Operation - Initialization on Creation/Change (BD_COBADICFL_MFGORDER_OPR_INIT)

Manufacturing Order Component - Initialization on Creation/Change (BD_COBADICFL_MFGORDER_CMP_INIT)

Reservation Document Item (RESERVATIONDOCUMENTITEM)

You can use this BAdI to fill custom fields on order component (reservation) level when creating or changing an order. This BAdI is called once at the beginning of order processing.

Please try that out and let us know the results. If it didn't solve, I can try to replicate in my system 

Thanks

Jerry

entry.title: Re: O-Data Service using CDS and BOPF
entry.content:
Hi Mahesh,

I hope this message finds you well.

I have successfully created an entity in SAP OData for tracking employee attendance. The current API I developed provides an overall count of attendance records filtered by a specified timestamp.

To enhance this functionality, I would like to extend the API to also include the Employee ID and Status in the response. This will provide a more comprehensive view of the attendance data, allowing us to see not just the count but also which employees were present and their respective statuses during the specified timeframe.

Below is an excerpt of the code used in the method TIMESHEETTABELLE_GET_ENTITYSET, which is part of the current implementation:

''''''method TIMESHEETTABELLE_GET_ENTITYSET.
*"select the record from DB
*data : et_entityset type zcl_z_hg_odata_mpc_ext=>TT_TIMESHEETTABELLE.
DATA: lt_filter_select_options TYPE /iwbep/t_mgw_select_option,
ls_filter TYPE /iwbep/s_mgw_select_option,
lt_filter_range TYPE TABLE OF /iwbep/s_cod_select_option,
ls_filter_range TYPE /iwbep/s_cod_select_option.

LOOP AT it_filter_select_options INTO ls_filter.
LOOP AT ls_filter-select_options INTO ls_filter_range.

CASE ls_filter-property.
WHEN 'AbholUhrzeit'.
data(lv_time) = ls_filter_range-low.
APPEND ls_filter_range to lt_filter_range.
endcase.
ENDLOOP.
ENDLOOP.
''''''''''''

I believe this enhancement will significantly improve the utility of our attendance tracking system. Could you please help me in incorporating these changes into the existing API?

Thank you for your continued support.

Best regards,
Deepika

entry.title: Re: SAP CAL system - F4 search - Internal error - Table format
entry.content: Goto Help->Setting->F4 Help Then select Dialog'(modal) in display

entry.title: Re: SAP CAL system - F4 search - Internal error - Table f...
Re: ABAP on Cloud Package no longer exists
entry.content: Having this issue now. Just suddenly wasn't able to log in. I have still not been able to resolve it.

entry.title: Re: What is the time zone response of SAP when communicat...
entry.content:
Hi

By product standards, the database datetime or timestamps (or any date/time) should be in UTC. There are/maybe a few exceptions, but that is the design standard.

Expectation is the frontend UI components would handle the conversation as they would know the client timezone (typically it comes from the browser).

If you are developing in BTP and calling S/4HANA Cloud, public edition, you would be going through public APIs.

You can also check the api specification on the SAP Business Accelerator Hub. For example, for Sales Order A2X, Get of the sales order header, 

since you mentioned the Maintenance Order:

 

If you are seeing otherwise, you should create a support ticket.

I hope this helps

Thank you

Jerry

entry.title: Re: How to include a URL to access the SAP WF approval sc...
entry.content:
Hi, there is a deep link to My Inbox with composing e-mail templates available with ABAP Platform 2020 SP02 The CDS view I_WorkflowTaskURL provides the deep link (WorkflowTaskURL) for a workflow task instance (WorkflowTaskInternalID).

The construction of the URL relies on different parameters which have to be maintained in table SWP_URL_CONFIG(maintenance view SWP_URL_CONFIG_V), before the WorkflowTaskURL will be generated with CDS view I_WorkflowTaskURL. The URL is not generated unless an entry with ID = MYINBOX is maintained. You will also find an according IMG activity under SAP Customizing Implementation Guide > SAP NetWeaver > Application Server > Business Management > SAP Business Workflow > Basic Settings (Workflow System) > Maintain URL Settings

entry.title: Re: Mapping - Concat repeating elements
entry.content: Thank You, I will give it a try.

entry.title: Re: SAP Result Analysis data migration from Legacy (Non S...
entry.content:
From what I understand, you are doing the first settlement in the ECC, correct?

If this is the case, I think there is no WIP to "adjust" in the settlement since it is the first settlement. The adjustment would be made if it were not the first settlement because with each settlement, the values are "adjusted."

Check how this WIP amount (800) was posted in period 7. If it was not posted by the RA, I think it will not be "seen" by RA.

Regards
Marcio Blos

entry.title: Re: CPI Message mapping problems with arrays
entry.content:
Hello Ryan,

First of all thanks for your support. 
So you are saying I can use the XSLT script before the IA mapping to get the desired xml and then do the mapping.

That means I should create the XSLT mapping before my message mapping correct ? 
I just want to make sure I understood what you are referring to.

Thanks again!

entry.title: Re: is it possible to install SAP Cloud ALM no cloud but on-promise (security defense requires it)?
entry.content: Paul - By definition, SAP Cloud ALM is a SaaS based product so an On-Prem version will not be available. However, you might review the Data Protection and Privacy Policy available via: https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-alm/setup-administration/data-protection-privacy Additionally, you can review the Compliance and Security Policies via the SAP Trust Center https://www.sap.com/about/trust-center.html As SAP Cloud ALM is a BTP Tenant, look for certifications for the Business Technology Platform.

entry.title: Parallel Processing of an report
entry.content:
Hello experts,
does anyone know if there is a way to call an Report parallel multiply times. I call my report with different parmeters, but because it is so slow it is not enough time to call  one after the other. I have to call it 11 times in the timeperiod of 2 hours. 

SUBMIT zsm_inkonsistente_szenarien3
  WITH p_start = 0
  WITH p_end   = 500
  AND RETURN.

SUBMIT zsm_inkonsistente_szenarien3
  WITH p_start = 501
  WITH p_end   = 1000
  AND RETURN.

entry.title: Re: SAP Community - Where are you from?
entry.content:
@kepo so great to meet you. I'm Stephanie and I live outside of Philadelphia (USA.) You are in a great area of SAP. So many will learn from your experience and vice versa. Are you planning to attend SAP TechEd Oct 8 & 9. If you aren't aware yet the catalog came out today.  Session Catalog

Do you want to share your TechEd playlist in the TechEd Group? SAP TechEd - SAP Community

It can help others who are thinking to learn S/4HANA Public Cloud! Best of luck.

entry.title: Research & Development Engineering in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408
entry.content:
I am happy to share the Research & Development Engineering innovations for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 release. Let's start with the highlight overview video: 

Now let's shift to the details and start with an important innovation in SAP Product Lifecycle Management integration with SAP S/4HANA Cloud. 

SAP Product Lifecycle Management Solution Integration with SAP S/4HANA Cloud

Before we go into the details, let's look at the overall strategy: 

From a strategic point of view, we simplified into two scope items 6U3 (integration through SAP PLM) and 6U2 (Integration external PLM systems). With 2408 we enhanced the 6U3 process with the SAP Product Lifecycle Management Solution Integration with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. 

With SAP Product Lifecycle Management solution, you can seamlessly transfer engineering bills of material (EBOMs) from the SAP Product Lifecycle Management solution and continue to work on them as manufacturing bills of material (MBOMs) in the relevant Maintain Bill of Material (Version 2) app. To allow you to transfer the BOMs and monitor the process, the following apps are now available on the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Launchpad:

Configure Handovers

In the Configure Handovers app the following attributes are defined. First the BOM Usages are configured for the handover process: 

Then the Item Categories are defined: 

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition we have already standard defined item categories.

Last, but not least, the plants are configured for the handover process. The plant is configured to define at which plant you want to manufacture your product or how the target BOM is to be used: 

Hand Over Engineering Bills of Material (EBOM)

In the Hand Over Engineering Bills of Material (EBOM) the magic happens. In the main screen of the Fiori app the SAP S/4HANA BOMs are visible as well as the SAP PLM BOMs: 

 When going into the details (object page), you may see the Source BOM and the Target BOM: 

Interesting for you is that the Routing has been integrated: 

By selecting the component in the BOM you can view the routing: 

A new routing can also be assigned:  

Actions can be taken on the Source BOM side: 

As well as on the Target BOM side: 

You may also add 3D models and pictures as well: 

Changes can be seen in the Log: 

Messages or Monitoring can be seen here: 

By clicking on the Show Details, you will jump into the Task Monitoring for Handovers app. This will be explained next. 

Task Monitoring for Handovers

The Task Monitoring for Handovers app monitors the handover process from SAP PLM to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition: 

In the object page, you may monitor further details: 

 

SAP PLM Integration to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Demo

The following shows a short demo for the SAP PLM to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition integration process: 

 Click on this link for further onboarding and also check out this user guide. 

New App in BOM Management: Maintain Bills of Material (Version 2)

With this new app (F1813A), you can create, update, or delete BOMs.

This app is a successor to the Maintain Bill of Material (F1813) app. In this old app, the copy from EBOM to MBOM happened with the button "Handover to Manufacturing". 

 This can be done in the new app with the "Copy BOM" button: 

The real "Handover" process may be done via SAP PLM as described above in this blog post, or also with external PLM systems via scope item 6U2

Maintain Subitems in Manage Order Bill of Material (Version 2) App

This feature enables you to create, update, or delete subitems for a BOM.

 The maintenance of the subitems quantity is integrated with the BOM components.

New App in BOM Management: Maintain WBS Bills of Material

With this app, you can create, update, or delete work breakdown structure (WBS) BOM.

You may work on an engineer-to-order scenario with projects. A project can have several work breakdown structure (WBS) elements that are nothing, but steps or different requirements involved in conceptualizing a finished product. Several activities that belong together can be grouped into WBS elements. You can create WBS BOMs for each of these WBS elements and carry out project-specific modifications.

Usability Feature in Manage Multilevel Bill of Material App

This feature enables you to copy and paste BOM item data from one BOM assembly to another thus reducing the tedious task of recreating the same BOM item data.

Manage Change Record (Version 2) App

Manage Change Record (Version 2) app is the successor of the Manage Change Record app. 

It provides continuous enhancement opportunities, improved performance, better usability, and supports Flexible Workflow only. 

It is recommended to use Manage Change Records (Version 2), as it is the successor of Manage Change Records. It is more simplified and supports the following objects:

  • Bill of Material (BOM)

  • Document (DOC)

  • Materials (MAT)

  • Production Routings (PRG)

  • Class (CLS)

  • Characteristic (CHR)

  • Sales Order Bill of Material (OBM)

Flexible Workflow

Flexible workflow allows businesses to run the Change Management process with transparency while taking appropriate decisions as you may check and approve process changes in a Change Record.

A new workflow can be created, or an existing one can be copied and edited to create a new one with the Workflows for Change Record app. 

Don’t hesitate to leave a comment and check out our community page to ask your questions and engage with the experts. Follow the PSCC_Enablement tag to stay up to date with our latest blog posts.

Follow us on @SAP and #S4HANA, and myself on LinkedIn.

From July 8 to July 12, we hosted a series of 20 compelling live sessions to highlight the exciting innovations shipped with the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 release. Missed the live sessions? We've got you covered! Don't miss this chance and watch them on demand–anywhere, anytime.

🔎Find All of Our Assets

SAP S/4HANA Cloud is the foundation of the intelligent, sustainable enterprise and is an innovative, robust, and scalable ERP. We at Cloud ERP Product Success and Cloud Co-Innovation offer a service as versatile as our product itself. Check out the numerous offerings our team has created for you below:

entry.title: Re: Action [core] page-editor/create-custom-section failed with timeout. Please report issue.
entry.content: Hello Sanya_Govil, There is one known bug in Page Map, but fix is not released yet. Could you please check that 'sap.fe.templates' library is listed in 'manifest.json'?. Like: ``` { "sap.ui5": { "dependencies": { "minUI5Version": YOUR_VERSION, "libs": { "sap.m": {}, "sap.ui.core": {}, "sap.ushell": {}, "sap.fe.templates": {}, "sap.fe.core": {} } } } } ``` Is it listed for you?

entry.title: Transportation Management for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408
entry.content:
Exciting product updates coming your way for Transportation Management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408. 

 

Integration of Returns Purchase Orders with
Transportation Management

With this feature you can integrate returns purchase orders with Transportation Management. This allows you to perform transportation planning, monitor the execution, and settle the freight costs for a returns purchase order as part of the Return to Supplier process (scope item BMK).

Configuration

You need to configure the integration of returns purchase orders with Transportation Management using the following configuration activities:

  • Define Logistics Integration Profile

    For returns purchase orders, you must create a logistics integration profile. To do this, you can copy an existing logistics integration profile, such as S003 (manual planning) or S004 (external planning), and adapt it as required.

  • Define Transportation-Relevance of Purchasing Documents

    You activate the integration of returns purchase orders into TM by assigning the control key 0058 and the logistics integration profile that you created in the Define Logistics Integration Profile activity to a combination of the required purchasing organization, and purchasing group.

Currently Transportation management for returns purchase orders is not integrated with Warehouse Management.

 

Integration of Returns Purchase Orders with
Transportation Management - Demo 

Check out this short demo featuring the configuration steps and the process from our Early Release Webinar 2408: 

 

 

Delivery Deletion Initiated from Transportation Management

You can delete inbound, outbound, and customer returns deliveries in Logistics Execution (LE) from the Manage Freight Units app in Transportation Management (TM).

 

If a freight unit has been assigned to a freight order or freight booking, you must remove this assignment before you can delete the delivery.

 

Enhancements for Consignment Orders in the
Outbound Process

With this feature, you can benefit from an improved handling of consignment orders and consignment order documents in the outbound process.

In particular, the following improvements have been made:

 

Execution status as an indicator for creating and deleting consignment order documents

 
The execution status of a freight order now clearly indicates if a consignment order document is created or has been created for the local consignment items of the freight order:

  • When the execution status of the freight order is set to Ready for Transportation Execution, the system automatically creates a corresponding consignment order document for each of the local consignment orders. These business documents can be accessed from the Document Flow tab or the Items tab of the freight order.

  • When you add freight units to the freight order after the execution status Ready for Transportation Execution has been reached, a consignment order document is created by the system for these freight units.
  • When you change back the execution status of the freight order from Ready for Transportation Execution to Not Ready for Transportation Execution, the system deletes the consignment order document, and the consignment order exists again only locally in the freight order.

No further changes allowed after sending of ASN or checkout of freight order

When an advanced shipping notification (ASN) has been sent or when the freight order has been checked out at the source location (checkout status value of handling execution status has been set), you can't unassign freight units from the consignment order or cancel the consignment order document anymore. To be able to do so, you must first undo the goods issue for the corresponding delivery and revoke the checkout.

Deletion of the consignment order after removal of assignment to freight order

If you unassign a consignment order from a freight order, the freight units are automatically unassigned from the consignment order and the consignment order is deleted. If the freight units are replanned to the freight order later, the system creates a new consignment order document for them.

 

Freight Cost Calculation based on Material Freight Group and Transportation Group

With this feature, you can calculate transportation charges in a freight order or freight booking based on a material freight group and transportation group.

Maintain freight rates based on the material freight group and transportation group in the freight agreement during negotiations with the carrier. Also, consider the material freight group and transportation group for the respective freight documents in the freight cost calculation after creation of freight agreement. 

 

Billing of Freight Cost for Batch-Managed Material

​For transportation management scenarios that are initiated from a sales document, the freight cost of the freight document can be taken into account when the corresponding billing document is created. This functionality is also available for batch-managed materials now. 

 

 

Refinement of Freight Settlement Initiation for Advanced Intercompany Processes

​Freight order does not contain an "own" location [e.g. in advanced intercompany sales scenario with (internal) incoterm CIF, where the freight order of the selling organization relates to the transport from port of destination to the customer location].

Freight orders automatically qualify for freight cost confirmation and settlement process as soon as the departure from the source location of relevant stage is reported.

 

Enhancement of the Form Data Provider for the WAYBILL Output Type

​The Form Data Provider of the Output Type ‘WAYBILL’ was extended with details on the Shipper’s Contact and Container Number.

 

Form Data provider for the WayBill Output Type is extended with the following attributes:

  • Container Details:
  • Container Number
  • Container Country/Region
  • Shipper Contact Number
  • Shipper’s E-mail
  • Shipper’s Phone
  • Shipper’s Mobile Phone
  • Shipper’s Fax

 

Third-Party Warehouse Management with Transportation Management - Advanced Integration Scenario

​Support integrated logistics processes with a third-party warehouse management system (Scope Item 1ZQ) and transportation planning and execution (Scope Items 6W1, 6W2, 6W3).

 The improvements: 

  • Ability to distribute outbound or inbound deliveries that are integrated with transportation management to a connected, third-party warehouse management system.
  • Subsequent changes to deliveries after they were distributed to the third-party warehouse management system.

 

Warehouse Outbound Processing with External Transportation Planning - Advanced Integration Scenario

​Support integrated logistics processes with Warehouse Management (Scope Item 3BS) and transportation planning in an external Transportation Management System (Scope Item 6W1).

 The following was enhanced: 

  • Enable integration with Warehouse Management, if freight units are distributed and planned in a connected external transportation management system.

 

Task Tutorials + Learning Content for Transportation Management with 2408 Release 

​New task tutorials and updated learnings to showcase an end-to-end scenario in Transportation Management for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. Check them out: 

  • Main Learning Package

  • Manual Transportation Planning (6W2)

  • Transportation Execution (6W3)

  • Freight Settlement (6W4

  • Getting Insights into SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, Transportation Management

  • Implementing SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, Transportation Management

Don’t hesitate to leave a comment and check out our community page to ask your questions and engage with the experts. Follow the PSCC_Enablement tag to stay up to date with our latest blog posts.

Follow us on @SAP and #S4HANA, and myself on LinkedIn.

SAP S/4HANA Cloud is the foundation of the intelligent, sustainable enterprise and is an innovative, robust, and scalable ERP. We at Cloud ERP Product Success and Cloud Co-Innovation offer a service as versatile as our product itself. Check out the numerous offerings our team has created for you below:

entry.title: Re: Recurring Posting via Standard background job
entry.content:
Recurring Posting with automated background job

Recurring postings are postings that occur periodically over a given period of time. You need recurring postings if business transactions occur at regular intervals and have the same amount.

You can use recurring postings to perform G/L account postings and to post accounts payable and accounts receivable. Recurring postings include postings of rent payments, leasing rates, and insurance premiums.

Types of recurring postings provided by SAP.

  • G/L Accounts
  • Accounts Receivable
  • Accounts Payable

Business scenario: Rent expenses (voucher) should be posted every 5th day of the month with amount and other parameters specified in recurring document.

1.    Creation of Recurring document

Transaction Code: FBD1

  1. Input First Run on - It is the start date of recurring entries.
  2. Input Last Run on – It the is the date up to which the final recurring entry is to be carried out.
  3. Input Interval in Months (like 01- Per Month, 02- Every 2 Month)
  4. Input Run Date (Press F4 and Select 01 to 31)
  5. Input Document Type in document type SA/JV will use for GL posting
  6. Input Reference - It is a free field where we can give the invoice number which is printed on document.
  7. Input Doc Header Text – It is also free field where we can give the text as per requirement.
  8. Input Posting Key 40 & 50 for GL posting 21 & 31 for Vendor and 01 & 11 for customer. For full list Click “F4” on the field
  9. Input the GL Account number for both Debit and Credit Entry.

Recurring document posted and it will be used a template for each month posting.

Note: recurring document posted below is a statistical entry. Hence, it will not hit trial balance.

2.    Creating Variant for Recurring Posting

Transaction Code: F.14 & SM35

  1. Company Code – Enter the company code
  2. Settlement Period – Enter system date or entry date on which document will be posted
  3. Username- User ID from which recurring template/document is created
  4. Hold processed session- Tick always and save variant

Variant saved and will be used for background job in SM36 wherein settlement period will always be used as SY-DATE or system date.

In order, to post batch input session from SM35 we need to use program “RSBDCSUB” and save variant as show below

3.    Define Jobs for Recurring postings

Transaction Code: SM36

  1. Job Name – Recurring_Posting Job
  2. Job Class – C
  3. Maintain Step
  4. Name- Enter SAP program name for F.14 “ SAPF120” & SM35 “RSBDCSUB”
  5. Variant – Enter variant name created in Step#2
  6. Check and save

 

 

Click on start job

Thanks,

Manish Gupta

entry.title: SAP SOP Transfer plan from SIS
entry.content:
Hi 

I would like to clarify some thing regarding to SAP SOP (Sales and Operations planning).

I need to create plan, based on historical data of sold products. I can get this from table LIPS for deliveries with movement type 601.

Desired process is to run MC87 > Edit > Create sales plan > Transfer plan from SIS.

--------------------------------

So I created SIS structure withing following steps:

MC21 Create Self-Defined Information Structure
SPRO>Logistics - General>LIS>Logistics Data Warehouse>Updating>Updating Definition>General Definition Using Update Groups>Maintain Update Groups Update group -
SPRO>Logistics - General>Logistics Data Warehouse>Updating>Updating Definition>Specific definition using Update Rules>Maintain Update Rules>Display
MC24 Create update rule
OVRA Maintain Statistics Groups for Customers
OVRF Maintain Statistics Groups for Material
SPRO>Logistics - General>Logistics Data Warehouse>Updating>Updating Control>Settings: Sales>Statistics Groups>Maintain Statistics Groups for Sales Documents
SPRO>Logistics - General>Logistics Data Warehouse>Updating>Updating Control>Settings: Sales>Statistics Groups>Assign Statistics Groups for each Delivery Item Type
OMO1 Activate SIS Update
-------------------------------------
Questions are:
1. How to fill new SIS by old data?
2. When we create new deliveries and goods issue, should be new SIS table updated automatically. Or should we update it via  custom program or something else?

entry.title: Re: URGENT! Help Required - Not able to see LOCALE fields...
entry.content: No Not all LMS DB fields are available/exposed in SAC because it requires development to enhance certain aspects and develop the API calls accordingly in the backend. These would require enhancement requests whihc may mean some will never get included if there is not a big need for the particular data in the way SAC is designed to be used similarly like LMS standard audit reports vs UI or LMS BIRT custom reporting, or other Data Services, etc...

entry.title: Re: Action [core] page-editor/create-custom-section faile...
entry.content:
Hello Sanya_Govil,

There is one known bug in Page Map, but fix is not released yet.
Could you please check that 'sap.fe.templates' library is listed in 'dependencies' in 'manifest.json'?.
Like:
```
{
  "sap.ui5": {
    "dependencies": {
      "minUI5Version": YOUR_VERSION,
      "libs": {
         "sap.m": {},
         "sap.ui.core": {},
         "sap.ushell": {},
         "sap.fe.templates": {},
         "sap.fe.core": {}
      }
    }
  }
}
```

Is 'sap.fe.templates' listed for you? If 'sap.fe.templates' is not listed, then could you please try to add it in 'manifest.json' and recreate section after that?

entry.title: inbound IDOC FIDCCP02 - No subsequent document found in Accounting
entry.content:
HI

I we have set up an IDOC flow between S4 to ECC. IDOC Basic type FIDCCP02. Fi documents are posted fine but there are o - No subsequent document found in Accounting. Error message RW011. 

For other Idocs like Basic type'ACC_GL_POSTING01' it is working fine.

Manual posting are also fine.

I have checked the I_COMP and I_COMP_CHECK parameters to blank with user exit 008, enhancement F050S005, function module EXIT_SAPLF050_008 (transaction CMOD, see documentation.

I can create the sequential documents via OKBA/1KE8 in batch, but this is not how we like to do it.

 

Anyone that have faced this problem or have any ideas.

entry.title: Re: Create Extension Wizard to Create SAP UI5 Extension
entry.content:
Agreed. This is a chatgpt response. I also am looking for the promised "Create Extension" wizard in BAS and cannot find it.

I want to set the default value of a date field to today's date in a standard SAP List Report app F2876, and make the field read-only. But because the field is not bound to an odata context from backend (it's a local JSON model field used for input), the Adaptation Project does not give me the hooks I need to manipulate this field. 

But I cannot find the "Create Extension" wizard that the help document mentions. They don't say where it is, they just say use it.

entry.title: Re: Attachment transfer from PO to DSN - GOS / Business d...
entry.content: Yes, you can remove the header level attachment from the Idoc segments and just retain the line level attachments. Thanks

entry.title: Re: Using Job Scheduler in SAP BTP [14]: FAQs
entry.content:
Hi @prachidhingra,

I have tried the same pattern and it works fine. Can you check that you are using "Cron" type?

The format given by you (* * * * 8:12 */30 0) means that it will execute from the 8th to the 12th hour every 30 minutes. This means: 8:00, 8:30, 9:00, 9:30, 10:00, 10:30, 11:00, 11:30, 12:00, 12:30 and then the next day again staring at 8:00

entry.title: Re: Creation of a flexible Headcount Time Series with Stories in People Analytics
entry.content:
Hi FranErle,

thanks for the great post 👍

I have one question regarding the following section:

The 2nd maintenance step is to assign the previously created entities for cust_LegalEntityReportingKeyDate (in Figure 6 called “Monthly” and “Yearly”) to the Legal Entity.

You can assign only one kind of reporting key dates in manage data to the legal entity. I have created three kinds of reporting key dates for monthly, quarterly and yearly. What does it mean for operations?

Is there any need to change the assignment in the legal entity if you want to change e.g. from monthly to yearly reporting if you have currently assigned monthly to the legal entity?

Does the assignment in legal entity determins the kind of reporting dates?

Thanks in advance.

BR

Dirk

entry.title: Re: All permission removed after adding common super doma...
entry.content:
Hello all,

If my understanding is correct, this scenario should be related to a known issue that we had some weeks ago related to the new RBP UI, which removed assigned permissions when editing/adding new permissions into a role via new UI.

This issue was identified and fixed by our Engineering team in Preview instances on 18th June and 20th June in Production.

Root Cause: 

As there are dirty data, the response data structure is different from normal case, which has not been handled from UI side.

The team was able to restore affected roles via script based on Change History data at that time for reported customers.

In case there is a need to restore these roles, we'd need to check the change history and prepare the restore script as there is no automatic restore way. This should be addressed via Support Ticket under LOD-SF-PLT-RBP component. Another option is going to the legacy Role-Based permission page and manually restore the permission for these affected roles via UI.

Note: in case a support case is needed, kindly provide complete information, including:

  • Detailed description of what happened and when it happened
  • Company ID and Support Access
  • Affected permission roles

 

I hope this helps!

entry.title: Re: BAdi (business context) for Check print form
entry.content:
Hello,

if you need to customize the check, you can use Output management. You can modify the check output with installing the software Adobe Lifecycle designer.

Please refer to the following blog:

https://blogs.sap.com/2017/11/24/output-management-customize-content-form-for-outgoing-check/

Regards,

Yanica

entry.title: Re: Outside of Simulation in pMRP
entry.content: Hello,

entry.title: Is it possible to create Threshold based on the Dimension values as text instead of numbers?
entry.content: Our table does not have numerical values but a set of 8 text statuses. and we wanted to dynamically colour code the cells when new data was updated instead of manually performing the operation. Is there a way to do so?

entry.title: Re: Outside of Simulation in pMRP
entry.content:
Hello

System probably backward scheduled the replenishment proposals to calculate the start date, and it is outside the simulation start date. 

Please try to extend your simulation and try again.

Regards,
Caetano

entry.title: Re: Creating PIR for pMRP
entry.content:
Hello

You can create Inactive PIRs (uncheck the Active button), so that they are not relevant for MRP before you run pMRP and validate if your forecast is feasible. You can make changes in your demand plan in pMRP, and then release the PIRs when you are done.

Regards,
Caetano

entry.title: Re: locked for automatic sourcing
entry.content:
Hello

Your production version will not be selected by MRP, but you can still use it in production orders.

Regards,
Caetano

entry.title: Re: Could not get the ApiHub resource data
entry.content: Hi, peter. Yes, there is the Developing Full-Stack Applications Using Productivity Tools in SAP Business Application Studio Learning Journey. In that journey, there is a lesson about using external sources and adding it to your CAP service. Here's the link to the lesson: https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/develop-full-stack-applications-using-productivity-tools-in-sap-business-application-studio/consuming-an-external-service-from-sap-s-4hana_faa318cd-9b48-48c4-b5fc-e6c205fb7685. After that lesson, there's comes one where they create the UI app with SAP Fiori. I hope you find this useful.

entry.title: Re: Create a PI doc after confirmation WT
entry.content:
Hi 

User zero stock check or low stock check. It may fulfil your requirement

 

Regards

Rashid Khan

entry.title: Re: ByDesign 1808 Release: Analytical Report - Approval Task Details
entry.content: This data source seams to only contain information about the current approval step. I have a PO with multiple approval steps defined, but only the first step is included in this report. Is there a way of adding all the future steps as well?

entry.title: Re: Task 2 - CDS View Entity Associations ( August Developer Challenge )
entry.content: CDS entity data preview

entry.title: Re: Level-based hierarchy - in SAC with Live Connection
entry.content: Can anyone please confirm that Parent Child Hier in Calculation View using Business Application Studio and Live-Connection in SAC should work. I am facing error while trying to create a model based on the Live-Data connection in SAC. Here is the error: Server Error: Error [Server]: (#42208) Can't read the view : DBSHARED:DBSHARED/CV_TPC_VARIANCE_TOTAL_REP_COPY::[], reason: Hierarchy error Correlation ID: 27820405-1718-4634-b694-430803931819

entry.title: Re: API for EWM Change Material/Batch
entry.content:
Hello @jeffblan 

I think you should only update batch classification with inspection results e.g. copper content. Material classification should not be affected by that as it is valid for all the batches and copper content will definitely vary from batch to batch.

QM even in the public cloud provides integration between inspection characteristics and batch classification. Thus result recorded during QM inspection can be automatically reflected in the batch classification - see SAP Help Control Indicators for Inspection Characteristics So I think you don't need to reinvent the wheel and implement the requirement in standard.

If that doesn't work for you and you still need to update batch classification manually, use Batch Master Record API to update batch classification.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: API for EWM Change Material/Batch
entry.content:
It is not the batch classification I'm trying to update.

I'm trying to update the material/batch on the Handling unit.

It is a material-to-material movement but needs to be done on the EWM Handling Unit.

 

Jeff

entry.title: Integrating PDF for Outbound Delivery
entry.content:
Hello,

we are looking for a way how to integrate PDF outputs for Outbound Deliveries to a 3rd party. I have not seen an API for this currently. There is the option to add EDI as an output channel for Outbound Delivery, but there is no scope item or documentation for this process.

Does anyone have any experience they could offer?

Thank you

Adela

entry.title: Re: profit center allocation
entry.content:
Hello,

The profit center postings don't contain reference to cost objects and universal allocations won't allow you to use G/L account types besides N (non operating expense/income) and X (Balance sheet accounts). Primary and secondary cost elements can't be used because they require a cost object reference.

You can use Primary and Secondary cost elements part of the Profit center allocations only when sender side uses Posted amounts and basis of the received uses Variable Portions.

Without seeing the whole set up of the allocation which you are trying to do, i am assuming that the issue is explained in the below note. 

Please refer to the following SAP Knowledge Base Article for more details.

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3114399

Regards,

Yanica

entry.title: Choose your own adventure: The SAP TechEd Virtual session catalog is live!
entry.content:
I don’t know about you, but my excitement is building for the upcoming SAP TechEd Virtual event – especially now that the session catalog is officially live! You know what that means, Tech friends – it’s time to check out all the offerings at sapteched.com and block your calendars for some serious, roll-up-your-sleeves learning.

Join us online October 8 – 9, 2024 and choose from over 100 sessions covering a wide range of topics to create your own agenda for a personalized SAP TechEd experience. Each day is filled with a number of different session types that cover topics from six unique tracks: SAP Business Technology Platform – General, Application Development and Automation, Data and Analytics, Integration and Artificial Intelligence and Digital Transformation with Cloud ERP. Bookmark the sessions that are valuable to you – and keep in mind that you can watch them both live and on-demand.

Start off TechEd Day 1 with a keynote in which our senior IT leaders and guests will reveal the latest news and announcements around top innovations and product developments. After the keynote, stick around to hear from SAP experts and partners who will share their own experiences and best-practices over the course of the two day event. Discover our different session types including Lectures, Track Intros, Jumpstarts, Keynote Deconstructed and Voice of Partner sessions.

There is so much to gain from SAP TechEd this year, I promise, you won’t want to miss it! Be sure to register for the SAP TechEd Virtual event by going here. And for those of you who want to continue your learning journey after SAP TechEd Virtual, please join us at one of the SAP TechEd on Tour stops or the SAP Insider EMEA 2024, a TechEd companion event. Learn more here.

Thanks, I hope to see everyone virtually or in person for SAP TechEd 2024!

entry.title: Not able to log in to trial account.
entry.content:
Hi, I can no longer log on to my BTP trial account. This is the error: "The uaa credentials of the service key used to create this project are no longer valid. Please create a new project using the ABAP Cloud Project Wizard."

I have seen others with this error, but I have still not been able to resolve it. 

Any help appreciated.

Thanks

entry.title: Re: SAP webi formula to calculate the hours and minutes b...
entry.content:
what I meant that the result is same, one was in the decimal (h) and the other is in (hh:mm)
There is no defined format for converting decimal numbers to hours:minutes. You use the formula I described in the link you follow and you will get the format you want.
Solved: Convert Solid number to 00:00:00 format - SAP Community

and here again the defined variables

TimeBetween:     =TimeBetween([T1];[T2];SecondPeriod)

HH:     =([TimeBetween]-Mod([TimeBetween];3600))/3600

mm:    =(Mod([TimeBetween];3600) - Mod([TimeBetween];60))/60

ss:      =Mod([TimeBetween];3600) - [mm]*60

TimeInFormat:      =FormatNumber([HH]; "00")+ ":" + FormatNumber([mm]; "00") + ":" +FormatNumber([ss]; "00")

entry.title: 2408 release- C4C screen to open when triggered from a Mashup in any other NGAP screen
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

In the 2408 release, a new feature has been introduced that allows the C4C screen to open when triggered from a Mashup in any other NGAP screen. Has anyone had experience with implementing this?

Thanks

Shuva

entry.title: Tracking Project Cost adjustments and Revenue recognition
entry.content: Hello, I was wondering if anyone knows of an SAP T code/query to identify when a WBS element's cost was changed, including the amount and date of each change. I have WBS elements that span multiple years, and sometimes during that period have multiple adjustments to their planned costs. When we update the cost, it triggers a revenue correction to bring the projected revenue back in line with the current margin. Revenue in this case is fixed/known at the time the project begins, but costs are estimated and updated as more information becomes available over the life of the project. The opposite situation is when a project is closed before reaching the planned costs, triggering a lump sum revenue recognition (with no additional cost). I am pretty new to SAP and I've asked around at my company, but no one is aware of any reporting that would provide this information.

entry.title: Im new in SAP NEP EWM, where I can modify an address for a Outbound customer Order
Re: Invalid or missing namespace for 'Schema'

Thanks & Regards,

Durgarao .

Hi Chandu
How did u solve this error?
I have also encountered with same issue.
It would be really greatful ,If you share the solution with me .

entry.title: Data Capture Form for Existing Candidates
entry.content:
Hello,

We are trying to create a data capture form for a career fair.  Form includes School/Major/Minor and other relevant information you would convey to a recruiter at a college career fair.  However, if a candidate already exists with a primary email in the system, they cannot submit the form, and the relevant information is not updated in the Candidate Profile Extension.  Is there a way to curve this primaryEmail functionality, or have it update the already existsing profile with the new information?

entry.content: JUST To clarify : I have the error message to put the reason in 201 screen but the field is not showing

We are currently using an ECC 7.0 system and would like to know if it is possible to perform SDI integration with HANA Cloud with this version. Is the SDI version compatible or supportive for integration with ECC 7.0?

Please suggest.

Thank you in advance for your support

entry.title: how to change the source of material group description in ME12/ME13
entry.content:
hello gurus

my question:

our material group in MARA-MATKL has 2 description levels in the help search table T023T / H_T023.

when displaying a purchasing info record, the material group is displayed and the description next to it is description1.

is it possible to change the source of the description to be displayed? the user wants description2

entry.title: Account Based COPA Report
entry.content:
Hi Expert, 

we have developed both Account and Costing based COPA in a S/4HANA System. After we have created a report for Account Based COPA in KE30, linking all report rows to different Accounts or Group Accounts. 

When I execute the report I do not see any values on the rows even if the accounts are populated in ACDOCA (for example coming from FB01 posting or Billing Document). 

The only data that I see are the data coming from allocations or settlement. Could you help me to understand why?

In my opinion if I have to settle or allocate costs and revenue to COPA, in order to ensure report works it's nothing different respect Costing Based COPA.

Thanks

Armando

entry.title: Re: Invalid or missing namespace for 'Schema'
entry.content: Update: In fact it just resolved that pop up with "missing namepscape for 'Schema'", but failed at runtime. What solved the problem was changing the ODATA V2 to V4 and using the v4 version of Northwind services.odata.org/V4/Northwind/Northwind.svc/$metadata

entry.title: SAP Cloud ALM: Project- and Task Management on Steroids
entry.content:
Abstract

Project- and task management software has become essential for teams and projects to improve organization, collaboration, and productivity. It is the foundation for transparency across all project activities. SAP Cloud ALM does an excellent job in this area and is not only able to keep up with the competition, but is in my opinion even more suitable for SAP projects. In this blog post, I want to look at the requirements for a good task management tool and then examine how SAP Cloud ALM fulfills them. If you want to get an overview of task management with SAP Cloud ALM first, I recommend the video [6] by @Jagmohan_Chawla.

Introduction

We all use tools like Todoist, Things, Apple Reminders or Microsoft To Do every day to organize our daily work. When it comes to managing projects with distributed teams, you need more powerful tools. These tools are often called task and project management or collaborative work management tools. Popular examples of this type of application are Monday, ClickUp, Alsana, Trello, Wrike, Jira, or Smartsheet [5]. Recent studies estimate that the market for task and project management software will grow from $7 billion today to $20 billion by 2030 [2].

There are some basic functions every tool should be capable of:

  • Task creation assign them to team members
  • Multiple view options like Gantt charts or Kanban boards
  • The ability to prioritize tasks, set due dates, and categorize them
  • Notifications and reminders

Some of the advanced features are particularly relevant to project managers:

  • Break down complex tasks into smaller subtasks and set task dependencies
  • Built-in time tracking capabilities and progress monitoring
  • Quality gates
  • Reporting and analytics

Let's take a look at what SAP Cloud ALM has to offer.

Task Management Features

SAP Cloud ALM has all the basics of comparable task and project management tools. A detailed description ensures that a task can be performed as the creator intended without further questions. The rich text editor in SAP Cloud ALM lets you create easy-to-read descriptions with different formatting, bulleted lists, character sets, and even tables.

You can assign more than 25 attributes to a task. In the Context area a task can be assigned to a scope, a solution scenario, a workstream, a deliverable or an application.

Task Attributes

In the Status and Planning area, you can map the estimated effort using story points. A task can be assigned to a release and a timebox - a phase or sprint. You have the option to manually set start and end dates or link them to a milestone or timebox. If a milestone or phase end date is moved, the task end date is automatically adjusted.

In other tools, you can assign a task only to a person (responsible). In SAP Cloud ALM, you can additionally assign a task to a team, role, or user type. Team members can then proactively take on tasks, or a subproject manager can assign them at a later time.

Multiple View Options and Dependencies

In my blog post SAP Cloud ALM: Requirements Management on Steroids [1], I have already explained some of the functions that are the same for both tasks and requirements. This includes visualization as a list, Gannt chart or Kanban board or the creation of dependencies between tasks.

In addition, complex tasks can be broken down into smaller subtasks. Changes can be tracked using the history; task templates help save time and ensure consistent descriptions of recurring tasks.

Notifications

SAP Cloud ALM provides two ways to notify users: In-App Notifications and email.

The bell in the upper right corner of the screen indicates changes that are important to you - for example, if you have been assigned a task. From here, you can navigate directly from the notification to the task.

In-App Notification

Another option is notification by email. For that, open the Preferences and select the objects for which you want to receive notifications.

Notification Settings

Note that only the objects that have already been assigned to you are displayed in the settings. For example, if you have never been assigned a defect, the object type will not appear. Here is a sample email notification:

E-Mail Notification

SAP Cloud ALM makes use of a technique known as deep linking. Deep linking refers to the practice of creating a direct link to a specific page or piece of content within a website or mobile app, rather than just linking to the homepage or a general section. The “Go to SAP Cloud ALM” button opens the associated task directly. This works with most URLs in SAP Cloud ALM, as long as the recipient has the necessary access permissions.

Comments

SAP Cloud ALM supports communication among team members through comments, project documents, and real-time analytics. In this snapshot, two comments have been added to a task.

Comments

 

Quality Gates

A quality gate is a predefined checkpoint or milestone in the project lifecycle that assesses whether the project meets specific quality criteria before moving on to the next phase or stage. It serves as a critical control mechanism to ensure that deliverables meet required standards and are aligned with stakeholder expectations.

SAP Cloud ALM makes it easy to create quality gates, define questions and answers and document the status. You can import quality gates from the SAP Activate roadmaps.

Quality Gates

My recommendation is to create a quality gate for each phase. To learn more about quality gates, read the blog post Safeguarding Quality: The Necessity and Strategic Usage of Quality Gates in Projects [8] by @preethishetty.

SAP Activate and Roadmap Tasks

What does a successful SAP project implementation look like? What tasks should there be, what deliverables, what phases? SAP Activate is the solution, a comprehensive implementation methodology designed to guide organizations through the deployment of SAP solutions, particularly SAP S/4HANA and other cloud-based offerings. SAP Activate roadmaps [7] offer a comprehensive view of your project teams' associated activities, deliverables, and tasks with accompanying accelerator assets in document and hyperlink format.

With SAP Cloud ALM, it has never been easier to use the contents of an SAP Activate roadmap in your own projects. Simply select the appropriate roadmap (there are currently 26). The content will be copied to your project. The imported tasks are called Roadmap Tasks. The knowledge and experience from thousands of successful projects has been incorporated into the roadmaps to give your project a jump start.

With other tools, you are still thinking about what your project should look like, what attributes it should have, and what tasks need to be completed. With SAP Cloud ALM, you are already successfully completing the first phases of your project.

Relations

The power of SAP Cloud ALM lies in its ability to create links to other objects. Tasks can be linked to:

1:N1:1
Other tasksRequirement
User StoriesScope
Process hierarchy nodesSolution Process
External URLsSolution Scenario
 Workstream
 Deliverable
 Quality Gate
 Application

This allows you to navigate to other objects quickly and easily. Above all, these links provide full traceability and allow the project manager to effectively control his or her project. The reports provided by SAP Cloud ALM make this particularly clear.

Analytics

The Project Overview is the perfect start page for SAP Cloud ALM. There are over 21 cards that each user can customize. It is also possible to view only the information that is relevant to me or my role. In the example, I have only shown the cards for the tasks.

Project Overview

In the Task Trend Analysis app, you can monitor the task trends based on statuses over a period of time by using the timebox filter.

Task Trend Analysis

Project Progress Reporting gives you a complete overview of all tasks in each phase, how many are planned, how many are completed, how many are overdue, and how many are left. If you switch from the table view to the chart view, you will be able to see the progress of the project in the form of a burn down chart. This allows you to see at a glance how much the current status of the project is deviating from the original plan.

Project Progress Reporting, Table and Burn Down Chart

These are just a few of the reports available. The task distribution report is also very helpful. You can find a complete overview in the SAP Cloud ALM Export Portal for Implementation [9].

Room for Improvement

Other task and project management tools have some features that are not (yet) available in SAP Cloud ALM. Did you know that there is a new release of SAP Cloud ALM every 14 days? Some gaps may be filled by the time you read this. In any case, it is worth taking a look at the product roadmap [10] to find out about planned features.

Here are some examples of possible improvements. Some tools are often more customizable, e.g. in terms of user-defined attributes for tasks. On the other hand, SAP Cloud ALM is already optimized for the implementation of SAP solutions. In some cases, tags are sufficient to replace customized attributes.

Other tools offer powerful automation features that help reduce manual tasks. Users can set up triggers and actions (for example, when the status of a task changes, notify team members). These automations can significantly increase productivity and will hopefully be available in SAP Cloud ALM in the future.

SAP Cloud ALM provides comprehensive API management capabilities that enable fast and direct integration with third-party and open source tools. However, understanding REST APIs and programming languages is required. Some project and task management tools offer integration with other popular solutions, such as Slack, Jira, MS Teams, or GitHub, that work out of the box and can be set up without any programming knowledge.

SAP Cloud ALM does not currently include resource and capacity management as some may know it from MS Project.

Conclusion

Collaborative work management is a game changer for today's projects. Task management in SAP Cloud ALM provides most of the functionality of comparable tools. The ability to load SAP Activate Roadmaps into a project makes SAP Cloud ALM the first choice for SAP implementation projects.

There are three other aspects that also speak in favor of the SAP Cloud ALM: 

  1. You get a complete application lifecycle management tool with requirements management, process management and authoring, test and defect management, documentation management, and change and deployment management. 
  2. You can use SAP Cloud ALM at no additional licensing cost. See [11] for details.
  3. SAP Cloud ALM for Operations [12] is also included, ensuring smooth business operations and improving the quality and performance of business process execution.

I hope I have been able to convince you to consider SAP Cloud ALM for your next project. Are you already using task management in SAP Cloud ALM? Let me know in the comments section and tell me about your experience with this.

References

[1] SAP Cloud ALM: Requirements Management on Steroids Blog post by Robin Schönwald
[2] Project Management Software Market Size, Share & Trends Analysis Report by Grand View Research
[3] Managing Roadmap Task Updates In SAP Cloud ALM for Implementation Blog post by @Nagar_Megha
[4] Cloud ALM for Team Task Management and Planning Blog post by @WernerJ 
[5] Top 10 Task Management Apps for Teams by Fliplet
[6] Deep Dive in Task Management using SAP Cloud ALM YouTube video by @Jagmohan_Chawla
[7] SAP Activate Roadmap Viewer
[8] Safeguarding Quality: The Necessity and Strategic Usage of Quality Gates in Projects Blog post by @preethishetty 
[9] SAP Cloud ALM for Implementation Expert Portal / Analytics SAP Support Portal
[10] SAP Cloud ALM Product Roadmap SAP Roadmap Explorer
[11] Usage Rights
[12] SAP Cloud ALM for Operations Expert Portal

entry.title: Re: Task 2 - CDS View Entity Associations ( August Developer Challenge )
entry.content:
Kindly find below the screenshot of Data Preview of the CDS View Entity.

Thanks.

entry.title: It is not possible to debug programs in SAP GUI for HTML
entry.content:
I am logged into my SAP session, on Chrome, and a simple program:

The program executes and runs, no problem. I cannot however debug this program, or any program for that matter.

All my settings are correct:

However when I try and set an external breakpoint:

I get the following message:

This is odd because I am trying to set an external breakpoint! Has anyone else had this problem and solved it in any browser?

All help is really greatly appreciated.

Kind regards, 

Warren

entry.title: Re: Invalid or missing namespace for 'Schema'
entry.content: What solved the problem was changing the ODATA V2 to V4 and using the v4 version of Northwind services.odata.org/V4/Northwind/Northwind.svc/$metadata

entry.title: Commission REST API Error
entry.content:
A colleague is using the Commission REST API to create a Contract version and is getting the following error in the Response:  {contracts":{"_ERROR_":"TCMP_09011:E: Attempted to modify a read-only value."}}

Unfortunately, the error doesn't tell us which value it's referring to.  Is there a way to determine which field/value is causing the error?

I can attach a copy of the Request Body if that would help.

Thanks in advance!

Ron

entry.title: How to make Serialized Material with AVLB status no longer consumable
entry.content:
We have some serialized materials that when issued out, have the status change to AVLB and the Plant, Storage Location and Stock Type cleared for that combination. This is as desired. However, SAP still allows user to perform a goods movement against that same material/serial # even though it has already been consumed and should no longer be available for issuance. 

Is there a way to prevent any goods movements for this scenario? Struggling to find anything in config.

entry.title: Re: It is not possible to debug programs in SAP GUI for HTML
entry.content: It looks like you are running it via the browser which it's telling you that you cannot do, but with the GUI you should be able to enable an external breakpoint that will initiate the debugger.

entry.title: Re: It is not possible to debug programs in SAP GUI for HTML
entry.content: what you are trying to do? why not it in normal gui?

entry.title: Re: CPI Message mapping problems with arrays
entry.content: Managed to make it work, thanks Ryan!

entry.title: Re: CPI Message mapping problems with arrays
entry.content: @manu_maca30 yes, correct... but you will also need to remember to alter your source schema for IA so it matches the target for the to_dealernr/dealerNumber portion, and map the source to target directly.

entry.title: Re: SAP webi formula to calculate the hours and minutes b...
entry.content: Thank you Mr. Ayman for your efforts

entry.title: Re: How to link a dispute case and its log?
entry.content:
Sorry for bumping this up, but maybe it could help somebody else that don't have developer access.

To link SMCG_T_CASE_ATTR and SRMPROTOCOL you need to get the field SCMG_T_CASE_ATTR-EXT_KEY and link with the number inside the brackets at the end of of the field SRMPROTOCOL-DISPLAY_NAME.

entry.title: Teams VLS for users without company email
entry.content:
We are looking to enable the Teams VLS integration with our LMS. However, we have a large user population that does not have company-provided email addresses, and have a fake email in the LMS. We know that the automatic recording of completions won't work for users without an email address. However, we were wondering if we would still be able to generate a Teams link for the VLS session.

This link could be distributed (offline) to the participants without email addresses, allowing them to still access the session.

entry.title: Re: Exploring the Frontiers of Retail: A Glimpse into AI's Potential with Our Vision Retail App
entry.content: Nice work

entry.title: uninstallation steps for SDI DP agent
entry.content:
Please share the uninstallation steps for SDI DP agent to perform for our local linux server!


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: uninstallation steps for SDI DP agent
entry.content:
Dear Team,

the steps for the uninstallation are described on our Help page. Please refer to the following article:

https://help.sap.com/docs/HANA_SMART_DATA_INTEGRATION/f4a54d019f624c799cbc276a798e588b/0442b122cb2940e1b64e8c247ff504db.html?locale=en-US

I hope my answer has been satisfactory!

Best regards!

entry.title: Re: Error: Failed to resolve dependencies while running SAPUI5 visual editor in BAS
entry.content:
Hi @patrik_spiess .

We are getting the same error in Fiori Launchpad, not trying to extend the app, just clicking on a link in the standard app Customers Overview. When the user clicks on a Sales Order in Open Sales card, it brings up that error, :

 

We are at 1.108.33 on-premise S4/2022. What would you suggest?

Thanks

entry.title: Re: Ordered Quantity in Item Master showing incorrect balance.
entry.content: Excellent I tried and it is working well. Thank you

entry.title: Re: GENERATIVE AI HUB SDK - Python Error
entry.content:
Thanks Mario! It worked, but I got the following warnings:

 

Library/Frameworks/Python.framework/Versions/3.12/lib/python3.12/site-packages/langchain_core/_api/deprecation.py:141: LangChainDeprecationWarning: The class `LLMChain` was deprecated in LangChain 0.1.17 and will be removed in 1.0. Use RunnableSequence, e.g., `prompt | llm` instead.
warn_deprecated(
/Library/Frameworks/Python.framework/Versions/3.12/lib/python3.12/site-packages/langchain_core/_api/deprecation.py:141: LangChainDeprecationWarning: The method `Chain.run` was deprecated in langchain 0.1.0 and will be removed in 0.3.0. Use invoke instead.
warn_deprecated(

 

Any thoughts?

 

Thanks again!

Leandro

entry.title: gross weight scale VK11
entry.content:
Dear all, 

I need to set up a price scale based on gross weight in VK11. Imagine that we need to set the shipping fees for sending product by DHL so:

1 KG --> 20 EUR

1,5 KG --> 22 EUR

2 KG --> 24 EUR

And so on.

I'd appreciate any idea or solution. Because I tried but it doesn't work.

Thanks 

Angie C

entry.title: Landing of Inbound Delivery in Queue.
entry.content:
Hi All,
i have following  requirement.
1) when an Inbound delivery is created it should be landed in Queue before it is distributed to EWM. Then i will analyse this Inbound delivery and resend the reactivate the Queue.
2) An Inbound Delivery of specific Document type should be  landed in Queue before it is distributed to EWM. 
Does anyone know how to achieve my requirement. please provide you suggestions or any guide.

I really appreciate your help.
Thank you,

Regards,
VG REDDY

entry.title: Re: Choose your own adventure: The SAP TechEd Virtual session catalog is live!
entry.content:
Hi!

I just wanted to confirm if there are any spots for external speakers at the SAP TechEd event?

entry.title: Re: Task 1 - Create CDS View Entity ( August Developer Challenge )
entry.content:
My solution:

1. Data preview of CDS view entity:

2. Logic in the ABAP class. In specific, the field ‘Status’ should only have values ( O, A or X ).

entry.title: Re: DMC Integration Message Dashboard Not Working
entry.content:
Hi,
were you able to find the solution for the status "Queued".

Im recently finding the same issue for non conformance - defects recording. 

KR,
Sayuj

entry.title: SlipStream Issue with QM02
entry.content:
Hi All,

I have an issue with a flavour created on SlipStream for QM02. The basic issue is the transaction gets "stuck" on one screen depending on a certain sequence of events. If I run in the original screen, no issues, no errors or warnings.

The flavour has hidden some tabs, and merged data from the "subject" tab into the "Reference Object" tab.

Steps:

Run QM02, select a Quality Notification

This is the original screen for reference

The flavour merges information from the Subject tab into the reference object tab. Hides the subject, items, and activities tabs

Starting in the Reference Object Tab, I click the Processing tab, then the Tasks tab, finally back to the Reference Objects tab. No issues. All works.

When clicking from the Task tab back to the Reference Object tab, this is the profiler details

It submits the Tab01 request, and runs the collect process for the merge.

However, if I repeat the transaction and miss the Processing tab (enter on Reference Object tab, click Tasks tab, and finally back to Reference Objects tab) the transaction gets "stuck" on the Tasks tab.

This is the profiler response in this scenario

It submits the Tab01 request but a Restore activation State event happens. Basically, it goes to Tab01 (reference object) but immediately switches back to the Tasks tab.

I have no scripts attached to any screen events. There are no errors or warnings thrown by the back end. No errors in the browser developer tool logs.

Anyone have any ideas what is triggering this "Restore activation State"?

Cheers

James

entry.title: How to activate 2LIS_03_BF in RSA5
entry.content:
Hello experts in SAP , Today have a one problem.

The detail is that I need to activate the stock extractor called 2LIS_03_BF for some extractions that I perform through Azure. I try to enter the RSA5 transaction and proceed to find the extractor and activate the source, but I do not get any change. This is in the development environment. I attach a photo of how it shows its status.

entry.title: Interest in Participating in the Digital Skills Initiative
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I hope this message finds you well. My name is nessma elhawary and I am truly excited about the opportunity to join the Digital Skills Initiative. i have been working in linux-plus in egypt as sap mm consultant for the past 2 years. Throughout my career, I've recognized the immense value of staying up-to-date with digital advancements and expanding my skill set in this fast-paced world. sharpening my technical expertise and staying competitive in the evolving digital landscape.I am confident that this initiative will not only enhance my current capabilities but also empower me to contribute more effectively to the digital transformation efforts within my organisation and beyond.

Thank you for considering my application, and I look forward to the possibility of joining this incredible learning journey. Please let me know how I can proceed with the registration process.

Best regards,

@David_Chaviano

entry.title: I Like, I Wish - We'd like to hear more from you!
entry.content:
We value your feedback and we want to hear more!

The I Like, I Wish forum, where we invite you to provide feedback on the platform, the course, and your overall experience with SAP Learning, will be available throughout the course.

Your input is important to us, as we use this to improve the learning experience in the future.

entry.title: Hello!
entry.content: Hello! I'm Mohamed and I'm excited to begin my SAP blogging journey.

entry.title: Re: SAP Datasphere Replication Flow from S/4HANA to Azure Data Lake
entry.content:
Hello! 

Is there a way to get data from Azure storage to Datasphere? Is it possible to integrate Azure file storage with DS? 

Thanks for any kind of information!

Thanks,

Ruslan

entry.title: Re: Clearing open items of Vendor and Customer
entry.content:
Hi @Cathy4_21

It seems you're using the 'Partial Payment' clearing rule. In the example I provided, I used the 'Residual Items' rule. You might want to try both to see which one works best for your situation.

entry.title: Re: Compensation issues troubleshooting step by step
entry.content: Hi @xavierlegarrec ,  Our budget value in executive review is not same as worksheet, kindly let us know what could be the possible cause.

entry.title: Re: Saviynt integration
entry.content: @adhanet Did you get a solution to this post?

entry.title: Mesclar dados OJDT JDT1 com a AJDT - Merge OJDT JDT1 data with AJDT
entry.content:
Olá,

Estou enfrentando uma demanda que envolve a mesclagem da consulta do saldo do plano de contas, que inclui os lançamentos contábeis das tabelas OJDT e JDT1, com a tabela de log de modificações AJDT, que é acessada dentro do lançamento contábil. No entanto, não estou conseguindo identificar as chaves relacionadas entre as tabelas OJDT e JDT1 com a AJDT. Tentei pela TransId mas os números não batem

Alguém já criou uma consulta semelhante ou pode me ajudar com isso?

English

Hello,

I am facing a demand that involves merging the account balance query, which includes the accounting entries from the OJDT and JDT1 tables, with the modification log table AJDT, which is accessed within the accounting entry. However, I am unable to identify the related keys between the OJDT and JDT1 tables and the AJDT. I tried using the TransId, but the numbers don't match.

Has anyone created a similar query or can anyone help me with this?

entry.title: I am trying to set a workflow rule to send email when the "Assigned To" field on ticket is blank.
entry.content: I am trying to set a workflow rule to send email when the "Assigned To" field on ticket is blank. In other words how does the query Unassigned tickets work. I tried to set condition with "Not Assigned", "Is Initial", "#" etc but none work.
